name: edge_bevel_single
description: Edge Bevel (mesh.bevel, edit-mode Edges, param `width`) on a SINGLE ISOLATED interior edge of a unit cube -- the simplest case, both endpoints are bare (only one selected edge touches each). Topology-diff parity against a frozen reference-editor capture: 8v/6f cube -> 10v/7f (5 quads + 2 pentagons), no extra cap polygon needed for a lone bare end on a closed mesh (the vertex's one untouched incident face absorbs both new corner vertices as a pentagon). Two cases pin the per-vertex law scales linearly with `width` (width=0.1 and width=0.2 on the SAME edge). This is the baseline vibe3d's bevelEdgesByMask already reproduces today (task 0304/existing mesh.d unittest); kept here as a topology-diff regression lock and as the anchor case for the harder cap cases in edge_bevel_loop/edge_bevel_corner/edge_bevel_open_end (task 0391).
source: Frozen reference-editor capture via the private capture harness's edge_bevel op (task 0391), cases edge_bevel_single_interior_w01/w02.json. Selected edge matches vibe3d's own makeCube() vertices 6=(0.5,0.5,0.5)/7=(-0.5,0.5,0.5) (mesh.d's bevelEdgesByMask unittest uses the identical edge) so this fixture doubles as an independent reference cross-check of that unittest's expected chamfer centroid (0,0.45,0.45) for width=0.1.

provenance.harness: private capture harness (reference-editor edge-bevel tool, driven headlessly, mode=inset)
provenance.notes: mode=inset was the correct reference-editor edge-bevel mode to match vibe3d's `width` param 1:1 (offset=value exactly) -- the reference editor's Width mode instead produces offset=value*sqrt(2) for a 90-degree cube corner, an easy trap; verified against mesh.d's existing bevelEdgesByMask unittest (chamfer centroid 0,0.45,0.45) bit-for-bit before freezing.
